evidence: As a SF buff, found this to be fun.
I like the 'defender of justice' genre.
Predictable story, but it's kind of fun saying, 'I knew that was coming!'
Pretty predictable plot, but in my opinion anything with Morgan Freeman in it is worth watching.
All the stuff guys love - guns, blowing things up, intricate escape, car chase - and all in the first 30 minutes!
I found the series entertaining; in fact, I 'binged' it in about a week.
Don't look for mind-blowing special effects from 1956! But it's about the story, not the visuals.
Although I'm not big on 'chik fliks' I watched this with my sweetie and thoroughly enjoyed it.
I won't watch any more of this unless they clean up the debris from dropping all those bombs.
Lots of guy blood and gore, acts of heroism, but pretty predictable.
A lot of violence, but surprisingly clean language-wise.
Timeless character study about the dangers of leadership.
One of the greatest lines is, 'Perhaps God has a better plan for my life than I do.'

summary: The user enjoys fast-paced sci-fi and action genres, often appreciating lighthearted entertainment with predictable plots. They show a moderate sensitivity to production quality and a mixed emotional engagement with the content.
